media.source: Scorched Tanks v1.75 from Amiga Power #41 disk 2
patches: S/Startup-Sequence autostarts SCORCHED_TANKSV1.75 from the AP41 disk root with LF-only Stack 65536
Injected ADF is mounted once through vAmigaWeb insert_file(0) with reset-on-load semantics
Launcher applies an A1200-style AROS diagnostic hardware profile for bundled AROS
Launcher auto-checks an intentionally absent hosted licensed Kickstart path before falling back to AROS
Launcher applies A500 OCS 512K chip + 512K slow, no fast RAM for user/host supplied Kickstart
Launcher records mount attempts, audio state, firmware path, hardware profile, and exact-game blocker

verification.exact_result: v1.75 AP41 media boots to the coverdisk menu with AROS in SAE. F2 launches the game, and the LF Stack 65536 A1200-style AROS profile reaches the Scorched Tanks v1.75 title/legal screen, then black-screens before gameplay after user input or extended wait. A500-style AROS returns to Workbench instead of gameplay. PlanetEmu v1.77, Aminet v1.85, and GameBase fixed v1.90 all launch AMOS and then fail under AROS with 0x0100000F memory-header/free-list corruption or black/empty-screen state before gameplay; exact GameBase A500 512K chip + 512K slow-memory AROS returns to empty Workbench. Mounting a second ENV: floppy stops the Workbench ENV requester but still opens an empty ScorchedTanks window instead of starting gameplay. v0.95 runs its own startup but A500-style AROS reports not enough memory, while larger chip/fast profiles black-screen or abort in SAE memory mapping. EmulatorJS/PUAE with A500/OCS/AROS fallback reaches Workbench only or an unusable emulator menu state. WinUAE 2.3.2 with AMOS-for-Windows AROS proves AMOS Professional itself can boot, but Scorched Tanks v1.90 still raises 0x0100000F Memory header not located; a 4 MB chip/no-fast diagnostic either raises the same alert or black-screens after Continue. Browser SAE with the older AMOS-for-Windows AROS ROM/ext also fails in Kickstart reset loops: v1.77 A500 reset 10 times in 90s, v1.77 A1200 reset 21 times in 90s, and v0.95 A500 reset 10 times in 90s, with flat non-game canvases. A v1.77 two-disk shim that adds AMOS-for-Windows amos.library to LIBS: still fails: A500 512K chip + 512K slow reports not enough memory, and A500 1 MB chip + 2 MB fast still raises 0x0100000F. Latest official AROS 2026-04-28 matches bundled AROS assets. GameBase and WHDLoad GenericKick recipes point to licensed Kickstart images rather than a proven free AROS path. No licensed local Kickstart 1.3 ROM was found on this host.
verification.compatibility_port_result: Live public browser proof at tinyassets.io hit Kade with a real canvas click, Big Shot, and WebAudio running; this is a labeled fallback, not exact original-game proof.
